parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces substantial changes to the retry, error-handling, and job-control subsystems. While test coverage exists for some new modules (retry, bigmodel-errors), the core client (glm-client.mjs) and job-lifecycle modules (job-control.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs) lack dedicated test files despite significant modifications. A deleted module (process.mjs) raises import-integrity risk. These gaps could allow silent failures in production retry/error paths that are hard to diagnose after deployment.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs module — potential dangling imports causing runtime crashes
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely removed (67 lines, 0 added). If any file in the codebase still has a static or dynamic import referencing './process.mjs' or 'process', it will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime. The check-imports.mjs script was modified (5 lines changed) but there is no evidence that a comprehensive grep for process.mjs references was performed across all entry points including hooks, CI scripts, and test files. A stale import would only surface in production when the specific code path is exercised.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs\|from.*process' scripts/ commands/ test-automation/` to verify no remaining references exist. Add an explicit CI guard that asserts no file imports the deleted module path.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs has 155 lines of changes but no dedicated test file
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs is the core HTTP client handling BigModel API communication. It was significantly modified (155 lines changed) to integrate new retry logic (retry.mjs, 203 lines) and error dispatch (bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines). No test file (e.g., tests/glm-client.test.mjs) appears in the diff. The retry and error-classification modules have tests, but their integration with the client — including error propagation, backoff timing, and fallback behavior — is untested. A misrouted error or incorrect retry condition in the client would cause silent job failures or hung requests in production.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration-level test file for glm-client.mjs that exercises at minimum: (1) successful request, (2) retriable error with backoff, (3) non-retriable error fast-fail, (4) max-retries exhaustion, and (5) network timeout handling. These should mock the HTTP layer, not the retry module.

findings.title: job-control.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs modified without test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) and scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manage job lifecycle state — creation, tracking, and completion. Neither has a corresponding test file in the diff. Job state corruption (e.g., a job marked as running after process crash) could cause duplicate work, stuck jobs, or lost results. The state.mjs module has tests, but the job-control layer that uses it does not.
findings.recommendation: Add unit tests for tracked-jobs.mjs covering: job creation, state transitions, crash-recovery (orphaned jobs), and concurrent access patterns. At minimum, add a test that verifies job state is consistent after an interrupted operation.

findings.title: Retry + error-classification interaction has no integration validation
findings.body: retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) are both new and have individual tests (352 and 189 lines respectively). However, the critical interaction — where bigmodel-errors classifies an error and retry.mjs acts on that classification — is only exercised through glm-client.mjs, which has no tests. A misclassification (e.g., treating a server error as non-retriable, or a client error as retriable) would be invisible until a real API failure occurs. The individual unit tests cannot catch cross-module contract violations.
findings.recommendation: Add contract tests that verify the error-classification-to-retry-decision pipeline end-to-end. Specifically: for each error type in bigmodel-errors, assert that retry.mjs makes the correct retry/abort decision. This can be a single focused test file that imports both modules.

next_steps: Verify no remaining imports of deleted process.mjs across the entire codebase
Add test coverage for glm-client.mjs covering retry integration and error propagation
Add tests for tracked-jobs.mjs and job-control.mjs focusing on state consistency
Add a cross-module contract test between bigmodel-errors.mjs and retry.mjs
Run the full UAT suite against the cumulative v0.4.x changes before tagging v0.4.7
